sections.title: How Computers Work
sections.summary: Understand the machine before you write the code.

sections.title: Getting Started
sections.summary: Build environment confidence, run the first programs, and make the terminal and editor feel safe.

sections.title: Language Basics
sections.summary: Teach values, control flow, and data structures as one zero-magic builder path.

sections.title: Functions and Errors
sections.summary: Turn inline logic into reusable function boundaries and make failure explicit.

sections.title: Types and Design
sections.summary: Shape programs with types, interfaces, composition, and text-heavy workflows.

sections.title: Packages and I/O
sections.summary: Teach module boundaries, package identity, CLI workflows, encoding, and filesystem work.

sections.title: Backend and DB
sections.summary: Teach the first application-shaped backend flows with HTTP and database work.

sections.title: Concurrency
sections.summary: Teach goroutines, channels, context, scheduling, and bounded concurrency patterns.

sections.title: Quality and Test
sections.summary: Teach verification, benchmarks, and profiling so learners can prove behavior and inspect cost.

sections.title: Architecture
sections.summary: Teach package design, boundary thinking, and service-shape reasoning.

sections.title: Production
sections.summary: Teach runtime behavior, logging, shutdown, and deployment-aware thinking.

sections.title: Flagship
sections.summary: Integrate larger project work and generation-assisted tooling into one final proof stage.
